  [user_task_description] # Requirements Document for 'SmartHomeManager' Web Application  ## 1. Objective Develop a comprehensive web application named 'SmartHomeManager' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to manage smart home devices, control them remotely, set automation rules, and monitor energy consumption. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.  ## 2. Language The required development language for the 'SmartHomeManager' application is Python.  ## 3. Page Design  The 'SmartHomeManager' web application will consist of the following seven pages:  ### 1. Dashboard Page - **Page Title**: Smart Home Dashboard - **Overview**: The main hub displaying overview of all devices, quick controls, and navigation to all functionality. - **Elements**:   - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.   - **ID: device-summary** - Type: Div - Summary showing total devices, active devices, and offline devices count.   - **ID: device-list-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to device list page.   - **ID: add-device-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to add device page.   - **ID: automation-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to automation rules page.   - **ID: energy-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to energy report page.   - **ID: activity-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to activity logs page.   - **ID: room-list** - Type: Div - List of all rooms with device counts, displayed as a dashboard section.  ### 2. Device List Page - **Page Title**: My Devices - **Overview**: A page displaying all registered smart devices with their status and quick controls. - **Elements**:   - **ID: device-list-page** - Type: Div - Container for the device list page.   - **ID: device-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying all devices with name, type, room, status, and actions.   - **ID: control-device-button-{device_id}** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to device control page (each row has this button).   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 3. Add Device Page - **Page Title**: Add New Device - **Overview**: A page for users to register a new smart device. - **Elements**:   - **ID: add-device-page** - Type: Div - Container for the add device page.   - **ID: device-name** - Type: Input - Field to input device name.   - **ID: device-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select device type (Light, Thermostat, Camera, Lock, Sensor, Appliance).   - **ID: device-room** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select room (Living Room, Bedroom, Kitchen, Bathroom, Garage).   - **ID: submit-device-button** - Type: Button - Button to submit the new device.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 4. Device Control Page - **Page Title**: Device Control - **Overview**: A page for controlling a specific device with detailed settings. - **Elements**:   - **ID: device-control-page** - Type: Div - Container for the device control page.   - **ID: device-name-display** - Type: H2 - Display device name.   - **ID: device-status-display** - Type: Div - Display current device status (Online/Offline).   - **ID: power-toggle** - Type: Button - Button to toggle device power on/off.   - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save device settings.   - **ID: back-to-devices** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to device list.  ### 5. Automation Rules Page - **Page Title**: Automation Rules - **Overview**: A page for creating and managing automation rules for devices. - **Elements**:   - **ID: automation-page** - Type: Div - Container for the automation rules page.   - **ID: rules-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying all automation rules with name, trigger, action, and status.   - **ID: rule-name** - Type: Input - Field to input rule name.   - **ID: trigger-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select trigger type (Time, Motion, Temperature).   - **ID: trigger-value** - Type: Input - Field to input trigger value (e.g., time or threshold).   - **ID: action-device** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select target device.   - **ID: action-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select action type (Turn On, Turn Off, Set Brightness, Set Temperature).   - **ID: add-rule-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new automation rule.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 6. Energy Report Page - **Page Title**: Energy Report - **Overview**: A page displaying energy consumption data and statistics for all devices. - **Elements**:   - **ID: energy-page** - Type: Div - Container for the energy report page.   - **ID: energy-summary** - Type: Div - Summary showing total energy consumption and cost estimate.   - **ID: energy-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying energy consumption per device with date and kWh.   - **ID: date-filter** - Type: Input (date) - Field to filter energy data by date.   - **ID: apply-filter-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply date filter.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 7. Activity Logs Page - **Page Title**: Activity Logs - **Overview**: A page displaying all device activity logs and system events. - **Elements**:   - **ID: activity-page** - Type: Div - Container for the activity logs page.   - **ID: activity-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying activity logs with timestamp, device, action, and details.   - **ID: search-activity** - Type: Input - Field to search activity logs.   - **ID: apply-search-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply search filter.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ## 4. Data Storage  The 'SmartHomeManager' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:  ### 1. User Data - **File Name**: `users.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|email   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|john@example.com   jane_smith|jane@example.com   ```  ### 2. Device Data - **File Name**: `devices.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|device_id|device_name|device_type|room|brand|model|status|power|brightness|temperature|mode|schedule_time   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|1|Living Room Light|Light|Living Room|Philips|Hue|Online|on|75|||Auto|   john_doe|2|Bedroom Thermostat|Thermostat|Bedroom|Nest|Learning|Online|on||72|Auto|22:00   jane_smith|3|Kitchen Camera|Camera|Kitchen|Ring|Indoor|Online|on||||Manual|   ```  ### 3. Room Data - **File Name**: `rooms.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|room_id|room_name   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|1|Living Room   john_doe|2|Bedroom   john_doe|3|Kitchen   jane_smith|1|Living Room   ```  ### 4. Automation Rules Data - **File Name**: `automation_rules.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|rule_id|rule_name|trigger_type|trigger_value|action_device_id|action_type|action_value|enabled   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|1|Morning Lights|Time|07:00|1|Turn On||true   john_doe|2|Night Mode|Time|22:00|1|Set Brightness|20|true   jane_smith|1|Motion Alert|Motion|detected|3|Turn On||true   ```  ### 5. Energy Consumption Logs - **File Name**: `energy_logs.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|device_id|date|consumption_kwh   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|1|2024-11-01|0.5   john_doe|2|2024-11-01|2.3   john_doe|1|2024-11-02|0.6   jane_smith|3|2024-11-01|0.2   ```  ### 6. Activity Logs - **File Name**: `activity_logs.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|timestamp|device_id|action|details   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|2024-11-01 07:00:00|1|Power On|Automation triggered: Morning Lights   john_doe|2024-11-01 08:30:00|2|Settings Changed|Temperature set to 72   jane_smith|2024-11-01 09:15:00|3|Power On|Manual control   ```  All files will be saved in the `data` directory to ensure organization and easy access. The format uses a pipe (`|`) delimiter for better readability and parsing. Different types of data will be isolated to ensure efficient data management and retrieval.

Dashboard Page\n- URL: `/dashboard`\n- Methods: GET\n- Description: Displays summary of user's devices and quick navigation buttons.\n- Data shown:\n  - User's devices list (from devices.txt)\n  - Navigation buttons to Devices List, Energy Summary, Activity Logs, Automation Rules\n- Template: `dashboard.html`\n- Elements:\n  - Header `<h1>Dashboard - SmartHomeManager</h1>`\n  - Navigation Buttons:\n    - Devices List (`id=\"device-list-button\"`)\n    - Energy Summary (`id=\"energy-button\"`)\n    - Activity Logs (`id=\"activity-button\"`)\n    - Automation Rules (`id=\"automation-button\"`)\n\n### 2. Devices List Page\n- URL: `/devices`\n- Methods: GET\n- Description: Displays a table listing all user's devices with device details.\n- Data shown:\n  - Devices data from `devices.txt`\n- Template: `device_list_page.html`\n- Elements:\n  - Header `<h1>Devices List</h1>`\n  - Table (`id=\"device-table\"`) with columns:\n    - Device ID, Name, Type, Room, Brand, Model, Status, Power, Brightness, Temperature, Mode, Schedule Time\n  - Buttons:\n    - Add Device (`id=\"add-device-button\"`) navigates to Add Device page\n    - Back to Dashboard (`id=\"back-to-dashboard\"`)\n\n### 3. Device Details Page\n- URL: `/device/<int:device_id>`\n- Methods: GET\n- Description: Details and control page for a specific device.\n- Data shown:\n  - Device info from `devices.txt`\n  - Current status and controls (e.g., On/Off toggle, Brightness slider)\n- Template: `device_detail.html`\n- Elements:\n  - Header `<h1>Device Details - {{ device_name }}</h1>`\n  - Device data fields displayed\n  - Control Buttons:\n    - Power Toggle (`id=\"control-device-button-{{ device_id }}\"`)\n    - Save Settings (`id=\"save-settings-button\"`)\n    - Back to Devices List (`id=\"back-to-devices\"`)\n\n### 4. Energy Summary Page\n- URL: `/energy-summary`\n- Methods: GET\n- Description: Displays energy consumption per device and overall.\n- Data shown:\n  - Energy consumption from `energy_logs.txt`\n- Template: `energy_summary.html`\n- Elements:\n  - Header `<h1>Energy Summary</h1>`\n  - Table (`id=\"energy-table\"`): Username, Date, Device ID, Consumption (kWh)\n  - Back Button (`id=\"back-to-dashboard\"`)\n\n### 5. Activity Logs Page\n- URL: `/activity-logs`\n- Methods: GET\n- Description: Shows device activity logs.\n- Data shown:\n  - Activity logs from `activity_logs.txt`\n- Template: `activity_logs.html`\n- Elements:\n  - Header `<h1>Activity Logs</h1>`\n  - Table (`id=\"activity-table\"`): Username, Timestamp, Device ID, Action, Details\n  - Date filter Input (`id=\"date-filter\"`)\n  - Apply Filter Button (`id=\"apply-filter-button\"`)\n  - Back Button (`id=\"back-to-dashboard\"`)\n\n### 6. Automation Rules Page\n- URL: `/automation-rules`\n- Methods: GET, POST\n- Description: Manage automation rules.\n- Data shown/edited:\n  - Rules from `automation_rules.txt`\n- Template: `automation_rules.html`\n- Elements:\n  - Header `<h1>Automation Rules</h1>`\n  - Table (`id=\"rules-table\"`): Rule ID, Name, Trigger Type, Trigger Value, Action Device ID, Action Type, Action Value, Enabled\n  - Add Rule Button (`id=\"add-rule-button\"`)\n  - Save Settings Button (`id=\"save-settings-button\"`)\n  - Back Button (`id=\"back-to-dashboard\"`)\n\n### 7. Add Device Page\n- URL: `/add-device`\n- Methods: GET, POST\n- Description: Form to add new device.\n- Template: `add_device_page.html`\n- Elements:\n  - Header `<h1>Add New Device</h1>`\n  - Input fields correspond to device data fields\n  - Submit Button (`id=\"submit-device-button\"`)\n  - Back Button (`id=\"back-to-devices\"`)\n\n---\n\n## Templates Plan\n\nAll templates rendered with variables passed from Flask. Use `url_for()` for linking.\n\n1. `dashboard.html` - Summary view with navigation buttons.\n2. `device_list_page.html` - Devices table and navigation.\n3. `device_detail.html` - Detailed device info and control buttons.\n4. `energy_summary.html` - Energy consumption report table.\n5. `activity_logs.html` - Activity logs with filter inputs.\n6. `automation_rules.html` - Rules list and controls.\n7. `add_device_page.html` - Form for adding devices.\n\n---\n\n## Flask Route and Function Names\n\n- `dashboard()` -> `/dashboard`\n- `device_list()` -> `/devices`\n- `device_detail(device_id: int)` -> `/device/<int:device_id>`\n- `energy_summary()` -> `/energy-summary`\n- `activity_logs()` -> `/activity-logs`\n- `automation_rules()` -> `/automation-rules`\n- `add_device()` -> `/add-device`\n\n---\n\n## UI Element IDs Summary\n\n- Buttons:\n  - `device-list-button`\n  - `energy-button`\n  - `activity-button`\n  - `automation-button`\n  - `add-device-button`\n  - `back-to-dashboard`\n  - `back-to-devices`\n  - `save-settings-button`\n  - `submit-device-button`\n  - `apply-filter-button`\n  - `add-rule-button`\n  - `control-device-button-{device_id}` (dynamic)\n- Tables:\n  - `device-table`\n  - `energy-table`\n  - `activity-table`\n  - `rules-table`\n- Inputs:\n  - `date-filter`\n\n---\n\nThis design specification meets the requirements for a Flask web application, providing clear route definitions, templates, UI elements with consistent IDs, and aligns with provided data file formats and content."})

paths\nUSERS_FILE = 'data/users.txt'\nDEVICES_FILE = 'data/devices.txt'\nROOMS_FILE = 'data/rooms.txt'\nENERGY_LOGS_FILE = 'data/energy_logs.txt'\nACTIVITY_LOGS_FILE = 'data/activity_logs.txt'\nAUTOMATION_RULES_FILE = 'data/automation_rules.txt'\n\n# Helper functions to load data\n\ndef load_users():\n    users = []\n    with open(USERS_FILE) as f:\n        for line in f:\n            username, email = line.strip().split('|')\n            users.append({'username': username, 'email': email})\n    return users\n\ndef load_devices(username=None):\n    devices = []\n    with open(DEVICES_FILE) as f:\n        for line in f:\n            fields = line.strip().split('|')\n            if len(fields) != 13:\n                continue\n            user, device_id, device_name, device_type, room, brand, model, status, power, brightness, temperature, mode, schedule_time = fields\n            if username and user != username:\n                continue\n            device = {\n                'username': user,\n                'device_id': int(device_id),\n                'device_name': device_name,\n                'device_type': device_type,\n                'room': room,\n                'brand': brand,\n                'model': model,\n                'status': status,\n                'power': power,\n                'brightness': brightness,\n                'temperature': temperature,\n                'mode': mode,\n                'schedule_time': schedule_time\n            }\n            devices.append(device)\n    return devices\n\ndef load_rooms(username=None):\n    rooms = []\n    with open(ROOMS_FILE) as f:\n        for line in f:\n            user, room_id, room_name = line.strip().split('|')\n            if username and user != username:\n                continue\n            rooms.append({'username': user, 'room_id': int(room_id), 'room_name': room_name})\n    return rooms\n\ndef load_energy_logs(username=None):\n    logs = []\n    with open(ENERGY_LOGS_FILE) as f:\n        for line in f:\n            user, date, device_id, consumption_kwh = line.strip().split('|')\n            if username and user != username:\n                continue\n            logs.append({'username': user, 'date': date, 'device_id': int(device_id), 'consumption_kwh': float(consumption_kwh)})\n    return logs\n\ndef load_activity_logs(username=None):\n    activities = []\n    with open(ACTIVITY_LOGS_FILE) as f:\n        for line in f:\n            user, timestamp, device_id, action, details = line.strip().split('|')\n            if username and user != username:\n                continue\n            activities.append({'username': user, 'timestamp': timestamp, 'device_id': int(device_id), 'action': action, 'details': details})\n    return activities\n\ndef load_automation_rules(username=None):\n    rules = []\n    with open(AUTOMATION_RULES_FILE) as f:\n        for line in f:\n            user, rule_id, rule_name, trigger_type, trigger_value, action_device_id, action_type, action_value, enabled = line.strip().split('|')\n            if username and user != username:\n                continue\n            rules.append({\n                'username': user,\n                'rule_id': int(rule_id),\n                'rule_name': rule_name,\n                'trigger_type': trigger_type,\n                'trigger_value': trigger_value,\n                'action_device_id': int(action_device_id),\n                'action_type': action_type,\n                'action_value': action_value,\n                'enabled': enabled.lower() == 'true'\n            })\n    return rules\n\n\n@app.route('/')\n@app.route('/dashboard')\ndef dashboard():\n    # For demo, pick first user\n    users = load_users()\n    if not users:\n        return \"No users found\", 404\n    user = users[0]['username']\n    devices = load_devices(user)\n    return render_template('dashboard.html', username=user, devices=devices)\n\n\n@app.route('/devices')\ndef device_list():\n    # Demo: First user\n    users = load_users()\n    if not users:\n        return \"No users found\", 404\n    user = users[0]['username']\n    devices = load_devices(user)\n    return render_template('device_list_page.html', devices=devices, username=user)\n\n\n@app.route('/device/<int:device_id>', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef device_detail(device_id):\n    users = load_users()\n    if not users:\n        return \"No users found\", 404\n    user = users[0]['username']\n    devices = load_devices(user)\n    device = next((d for d in devices if d['device_id'] == device_id), None)\n    if not device:\n        return \"Device not found\", 404\n\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        # For example: handle power toggle and brightness updates\n        power = request.form.get('power')\n        brightness = request.form.get('brightness')\n        # Update device data in file: (not implemented - stub)\n        # Here, just simulate by modifying device dictionary\n        device['power'] = power\n        device['brightness'] = brightness\n        # Redirect back to device detail\n        return redirect(url_for('device_detail', device_id=device_id))\n\n    return render_template('device_detail.html', device=device, username=user)\n\n\n@app.route('/energy-summary')\ndef energy_summary():\n    users = load_users()\n    if not users:\n        return \"No users found\", 404\n    user = users[0]['username']\n    energy_logs = load_energy_logs(user)\n    return render_template('energy_summary.html', energy_logs=energy_logs, username=user)\n\n\n@app.route('/activity-logs')\ndef activity_logs():\n    users = load_users()\n    if not users:\n        return \"No users found\", 404\n    user = users[0]['username']\n    activities = load_activity_logs(user)\n    # Filtering by date (optional)\n    date_filter = request.args.get('date_filter', '')\n    if date_filter:\n        activities = [a for a in activities if a['timestamp'].startswith(date_filter)]\n    return render_template('activity_logs.html', activities=activities, username=user, date_filter=date_filter)\n\n\n@app.route('/automation-rules', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef automation_rules():\n    users = load_users()\n    if not users:\n        return \"No users found\", 404\n    user = users[0]['username']\n    rules = load_automation_rules(user)\n\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        # render_template('add_device_page.html', username=user)\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    app.run(debug=True)\n"})

  [user_task_description] # Requirements Document for 'SmartHomeManager' Web Application  ## 1. Objective Develop a comprehensive web application named 'SmartHomeManager' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to manage smart home devices, control them remotely, set automation rules, and monitor energy consumption. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.  ## 2. Language The required development language for the 'SmartHomeManager' application is Python.  ## 3. Page Design  The 'SmartHomeManager' web application will consist of the following seven pages:  ### 1. Dashboard Page - **Page Title**: Smart Home Dashboard - **Overview**: The main hub displaying overview of all devices, quick controls, and navigation to all functionality. - **Elements**:   - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.   - **ID: device-summary** - Type: Div - Summary showing total devices, active devices, and offline devices count.   - **ID: device-list-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to device list page.   - **ID: add-device-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to add device page.   - **ID: automation-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to automation rules page.   - **ID: energy-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to energy report page.   - **ID: activity-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to activity logs page.   - **ID: room-list** - Type: Div - List of all rooms with device counts, displayed as a dashboard section.  ### 2. Device List Page - **Page Title**: My Devices - **Overview**: A page displaying all registered smart devices with their status and quick controls. - **Elements**:   - **ID: device-list-page** - Type: Div - Container for the device list page.   - **ID: device-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying all devices with name, type, room, status, and actions.   - **ID: control-device-button-{device_id}** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to device control page (each row has this button).   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 3. Add Device Page - **Page Title**: Add New Device - **Overview**: A page for users to register a new smart device. - **Elements**:   - **ID: add-device-page** - Type: Div - Container for the add device page.   - **ID: device-name** - Type: Input - Field to input device name.   - **ID: device-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select device type (Light, Thermostat, Camera, Lock, Sensor, Appliance).   - **ID: device-room** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select room (Living Room, Bedroom, Kitchen, Bathroom, Garage).   - **ID: submit-device-button** - Type: Button - Button to submit the new device.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 4. Device Control Page - **Page Title**: Device Control - **Overview**: A page for controlling a specific device with detailed settings. - **Elements**:   - **ID: device-control-page** - Type: Div - Container for the device control page.   - **ID: device-name-display** - Type: H2 - Display device name.   - **ID: device-status-display** - Type: Div - Display current device status (Online/Offline).   - **ID: power-toggle** - Type: Button - Button to toggle device power on/off.   - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save device settings.   - **ID: back-to-devices** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to device list.  ### 5. Automation Rules Page - **Page Title**: Automation Rules - **Overview**: A page for creating and managing automation rules for devices. - **Elements**:   - **ID: automation-page** - Type: Div - Container for the automation rules page.   - **ID: rules-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying all automation rules with name, trigger, action, and status.   - **ID: rule-name** - Type: Input - Field to input rule name.   - **ID: trigger-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select trigger type (Time, Motion, Temperature).   - **ID: trigger-value** - Type: Input - Field to input trigger value (e.g., time or threshold).   - **ID: action-device** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select target device.   - **ID: action-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select action type (Turn On, Turn Off, Set Brightness, Set Temperature).   - **ID: add-rule-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new automation rule.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 6. Energy Report Page - **Page Title**: Energy Report - **Overview**: A page displaying energy consumption data and statistics for all devices. - **Elements**:   - **ID: energy-page** - Type: Div - Container for the energy report page.   - **ID: energy-summary** - Type: Div - Summary showing total energy consumption and cost estimate.   - **ID: energy-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying energy consumption per device with date and kWh.   - **ID: date-filter** - Type: Input (date) - Field to filter energy data by date.   - **ID: apply-filter-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply date filter.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 7. Activity Logs Page - **Page Title**: Activity Logs - **Overview**: A page displaying all device activity logs and system events. - **Elements**:   - **ID: activity-page** - Type: Div - Container for the activity logs page.   - **ID: activity-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying activity logs with timestamp, device, action, and details.   - **ID: search-activity** - Type: Input - Field to search activity logs.   - **ID: apply-search-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply search filter.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ## 4. Data Storage  The 'SmartHomeManager' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:  ### 1. User Data - **File Name**: `users.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|email   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|john@example.com   jane_smith|jane@example.com   ```  ### 2. Device Data - **File Name**: `devices.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|device_id|device_name|device_type|room|brand|model|status|power|brightness|temperature|mode|schedule_time   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|1|Living Room Light|Light|Living Room|Philips|Hue|Online|on|75|||Auto|   john_doe|2|Bedroom Thermostat|Thermostat|Bedroom|Nest|Learning|Online|on||72|Auto|22:00   jane_smith|3|Kitchen Camera|Camera|Kitchen|Ring|Indoor|Online|on||||Manual|   ```  ### 3. Room Data - **File Name**: `rooms.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|room_id|room_name   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|1|Living Room   john_doe|2|Bedroom   john_doe|3|Kitchen   jane_smith|1|Living Room   ```  ### 4. Automation Rules Data - **File Name**: `automation_rules.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|rule_id|rule_name|trigger_type|trigger_value|action_device_id|action_type|action_value|enabled   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|1|Morning Lights|Time|07:00|1|Turn On||true   john_doe|2|Night Mode|Time|22:00|1|Set Brightness|20|true   jane_smith|1|Motion Alert|Motion|detected|3|Turn On||true   ```  ### 5. Energy Consumption Logs - **File Name**: `energy_logs.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|device_id|date|consumption_kwh   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|1|2024-11-01|0.5   john_doe|2|2024-11-01|2.3   john_doe|1|2024-11-02|0.6   jane_smith|3|2024-11-01|0.2   ```  ### 6. Activity Logs - **File Name**: `activity_logs.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|timestamp|device_id|action|details   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|2024-11-01 07:00:00|1|Power On|Automation triggered: Morning Lights   john_doe|2024-11-01 08:30:00|2|Settings Changed|Temperature set to 72   jane_smith|2024-11-01 09:15:00|3|Power On|Manual control   ```  All files will be saved in the `data` directory to ensure organization and easy access. The format uses a pipe (`|`) delimiter for better readability and parsing. Different types of data will be isolated to ensure efficient data management and retrieval.
  [design_spec.md] # SmartHomeManager Web Application Design Specification  ## Overview This Flask web application manages smart home devices, their activity and energy logs, and automation rules, providing a comprehensive UI for user navigation.  ---  ## Data Files - `users.txt`   - Format: username|email   - Example: jane_smith|jane@example.com - `devices.txt`   - Format: username|device_id|device_name|device_type|room|brand|model|status|power|brightness|temperature|mode|schedule_time   - Example: john_doe|1|Thermostat|Thermostat|Bedroom|Nest|Learning|Online|on||72|Auto|22:00 - `rooms.txt`   - Format: username|room_id|room_name   - Example: john_doe|2|Bedroom - `energy_logs.txt`   - Format: username|date|device_id|consumption_kwh   - Example: john_doe|2024-11-01|1|0.5 - `activity_logs.txt`   - Format: username|timestamp|device_id|action|details   - Example: john_doe|2024-11-01 08:30:00|2|Changed|Temperature - `automation_rules.txt`   - Format: username|rule_id|rule_name|trigger_type|trigger_value|action_device_id|action_type|action_value|enabled   - Example: john_doe|1|Morning Lights|Time|07:00|3|Turn On|Light|true  ---  ## Flask Routes and Pages  ### 1. Dashboard Page - URL: `/dashboard` - Methods: GET - Description: Displays summary of user's devices and quick navigation buttons. - Data shown:   - User's devices list (from devices.txt)   - Navigation buttons to Devices List, Energy Summary, Activity Logs, Automation Rules - Template: `dashboard.html` - Elements:   - Header `<h1>Dashboard - SmartHomeManager</h1>`   - Navigation Buttons:     - Devices List (`id="device-list-button"`)     - Energy Summary (`id="energy-button"`)     - Activity Logs (`id="activity-button"`)     - Automation Rules (`id="automation-button"`)  ### 2. Devices List Page - URL: `/devices` - Methods: GET - Description: Displays a table listing all user's devices with device details. - Data shown:   - Devices data from `devices.txt` - Template: `device_list_page.html` - Elements:   - Header `<h1>Devices List</h1>`   - Table (`id="device-table"`) with columns:     - Device ID, Name, Type, Room, Brand, Model, Status, Power, Brightness, Temperature, Mode, Schedule Time   - Buttons:     - Add Device (`id="add-device-button"`) navigates to Add Device page     - Back to Dashboard (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)  ### 3. Device Details Page - URL: `/device/<int:device_id>` - Methods: GET - Description: Details and control page for a specific device. - Data shown:   - Device info from `devices.txt`   - Current status and controls (e.g., On/Off toggle, Brightness slider) - Template: `device_detail.html` - Elements:   - Header `<h1>Device Details - {{ device_name }}</h1>`   - Device data fields displayed   - Control Buttons:     - Power Toggle (`id="control-device-button-{{ device_id }}"`)     - Save Settings (`id="save-settings-button"`)     - Back to Devices List (`id="back-to-devices"`)  ### 4. Energy Summary Page - URL: `/energy-summary` - Methods: GET - Description: Displays energy consumption per device and overall. - Data shown:   - Energy consumption from `energy_logs.txt` - Template: `energy_summary.html` - Elements:   - Header `<h1>Energy Summary</h1>`   - Table (`id="energy-table"`): Username, Date, Device ID, Consumption (kWh)   - Back Button (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)  ### 5. Activity Logs Page - URL: `/activity-logs` - Methods: GET - Description: Shows device activity logs. - Data shown:   - Activity logs from `activity_logs.txt` - Template: `activity_logs.html` - Elements:   - Header `<h1>Activity Logs</h1>`   - Table (`id="activity-table"`): Username, Timestamp, Device ID, Action, Details   - Date filter Input (`id="date-filter"`)   - Apply Filter Button (`id="apply-filter-button"`)   - Back Button (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)  ### 6. Automation Rules Page - URL: `/automation-rules` - Methods: GET, POST - Description: Manage automation rules. - Data shown/edited:   - Rules from `automation_rules.txt` - Template: `automation_rules.html` - Elements:   - Header `<h1>Automation Rules</h1>`   - Table (`id="rules-table"`): Rule ID, Name, Trigger Type, Trigger Value, Action Device ID, Action Type, Action Value, Enabled   - Add Rule Button (`id="add-rule-button"`)   - Save Settings Button (`id="save-settings-button"`)   - Back Button (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)  ### 7. Add Device Page - URL: `/add-device` - Methods: GET, POST - Description: Form to add new device. - Template: `add_device_page.html` - Elements:   - Header `<h1>Add New Device</h1>`   - Input fields correspond to device data fields   - Submit Button (`id="submit-device-button"`)   - Back Button (`id="back-to-devices"`)  ---  ## Templates Plan  All templates rendered with variables passed from Flask. Use `url_for()` for linking.  1. `dashboard.html` - Summary view with navigation buttons. 2. `device_list_page.html` - Devices table and navigation. 3. `device_detail.html` - Detailed device info and control buttons. 4. `energy_summary.html` - Energy consumption report table. 5. `activity_logs.html` - Activity logs with filter inputs. 6. `automation_rules.html` - Rules list and controls. 7. `add_device_page.html` - Form for adding devices.  ---  ## Flask Route and Function Names  - `dashboard()` -> `/dashboard` - `device_list()` -> `/devices` - `device_detail(device_id: int)` -> `/device/<int:device_id>` - `energy_summary()` -> `/energy-summary` - `activity_logs()` -> `/activity-logs` - `automation_rules()` -> `/automation-rules` - `add_device()` -> `/add-device`  ---  ## UI Element IDs Summary  - Buttons:   - `device-list-button`   - `energy-button`   - `activity-button`   - `automation-button`   - `add-device-button`   - `back-to-dashboard`   - `back-to-devices`   - `save-settings-button`   - `submit-device-button`   - `apply-filter-button`   - `add-rule-button`   - `control-device-button-{device_id}` (dynamic) - Tables:   - `device-table`   - `energy-table`   - `activity-table`   - `rules-table` - Inputs:   - `date-filter`  ---  This design specification meets the requirements for a Flask web application, providing clear route definitions, templates, UI elements with consistent IDs, and aligns with provided data file formats and content.
================================================================================

# Requirements Document for 'SmartHomeManager' Web Application

## 1. Objective
Develop a comprehensive web application named 'SmartHomeManager' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to manage smart home devices, control them remotely, set automation rules, and monitor energy consumption. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.

## 2. Language
The required development language for the 'SmartHomeManager' application is Python.

## 3. Page Design

The 'SmartHomeManager' web application will consist of the following seven pages:

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Page Title**: Smart Home Dashboard
- **Overview**: The main hub displaying overview of all devices, quick controls, and navigation to all functionality.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.
  - **ID: device-summary** - Type: Div - Summary showing total devices, active devices, and offline devices count.
  - **ID: device-list-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to device list page.
  - **ID: add-device-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to add device page.
  - **ID: automation-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to automation rules page.
  - **ID: energy-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to energy report page.
  - **ID: activity-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to activity logs page.
  - **ID: room-list** - Type: Div - List of all rooms with device counts, displayed as a dashboard section.

### 2. Device List Page
- **Page Title**: My Devices
- **Overview**: A page displaying all registered smart devices with their status and quick controls.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: device-list-page** - Type: Div - Container for the device list page.
  - **ID: device-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying all devices with name, type, room, status, and actions.
  - **ID: control-device-button-{device_id}** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to device control page (each row has this button).
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 3. Add Device Page
- **Page Title**: Add New Device
- **Overview**: A page for users to register a new smart device.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: add-device-page** - Type: Div - Container for the add device page.
  - **ID: device-name** - Type: Input - Field to input device name.
  - **ID: device-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select device type (Light, Thermostat, Camera, Lock, Sensor, Appliance).
  - **ID: device-room** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select room (Living Room, Bedroom, Kitchen, Bathroom, Garage).
  - **ID: submit-device-button** - Type: Button - Button to submit the new device.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 4. Device Control Page
- **Page Title**: Device Control
- **Overview**: A page for controlling a specific device with detailed settings.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: device-control-page** - Type: Div - Container for the device control page.
  - **ID: device-name-display** - Type: H2 - Display device name.
  - **ID: device-status-display** - Type: Div - Display current device status (Online/Offline).
  - **ID: power-toggle** - Type: Button - Button to toggle device power on/off.
  - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save device settings.
  - **ID: back-to-devices** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to device list.

### 5. Automation Rules Page
- **Page Title**: Automation Rules
- **Overview**: A page for creating and managing automation rules for devices.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: automation-page** - Type: Div - Container for the automation rules page.
  - **ID: rules-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying all automation rules with name, trigger, action, and status.
  - **ID: rule-name** - Type: Input - Field to input rule name.
  - **ID: trigger-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select trigger type (Time, Motion, Temperature).
  - **ID: trigger-value** - Type: Input - Field to input trigger value (e.g., time or threshold).
  - **ID: action-device** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select target device.
  - **ID: action-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select action type (Turn On, Turn Off, Set Brightness, Set Temperature).
  - **ID: add-rule-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new automation rule.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 6. Energy Report Page
- **Page Title**: Energy Report
- **Overview**: A page displaying energy consumption data and statistics for all devices.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: energy-page** - Type: Div - Container for the energy report page.
  - **ID: energy-summary** - Type: Div - Summary showing total energy consumption and cost estimate.
  - **ID: energy-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying energy consumption per device with date and kWh.
  - **ID: date-filter** - Type: Input (date) - Field to filter energy data by date.
  - **ID: apply-filter-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply date filter.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 7. Activity Logs Page
- **Page Title**: Activity Logs
- **Overview**: A page displaying all device activity logs and system events.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: activity-page** - Type: Div - Container for the activity logs page.
  - **ID: activity-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying activity logs with timestamp, device, action, and details.
  - **ID: search-activity** - Type: Input - Field to search activity logs.
  - **ID: apply-search-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply search filter.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

## 4. Data Storage

The 'SmartHomeManager' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. User Data
- **File Name**: `users.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  username|email
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  john_doe|john@example.com
  jane_smith|jane@example.com
  ```

### 2. Device Data
- **File Name**: `devices.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  username|device_id|device_name|device_type|room|brand|model|status|power|brightness|temperature|mode|schedule_time
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  john_doe|1|Living Room Light|Light|Living Room|Philips|Hue|Online|on|75|||Auto|
  john_doe|2|Bedroom Thermostat|Thermostat|Bedroom|Nest|Learning|Online|on||72|Auto|22:00
  jane_smith|3|Kitchen Camera|Camera|Kitchen|Ring|Indoor|Online|on||||Manual|
  ```

### 3. Room Data
- **File Name**: `rooms.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  username|room_id|room_name
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  john_doe|1|Living Room
  john_doe|2|Bedroom
  john_doe|3|Kitchen
  jane_smith|1|Living Room
  ```

### 4. Automation Rules Data
- **File Name**: `automation_rules.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  username|rule_id|rule_name|trigger_type|trigger_value|action_device_id|action_type|action_value|enabled
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  john_doe|1|Morning Lights|Time|07:00|1|Turn On||true
  john_doe|2|Night Mode|Time|22:00|1|Set Brightness|20|true
  jane_smith|1|Motion Alert|Motion|detected|3|Turn On||true
  ```

### 5. Energy Consumption Logs
- **File Name**: `energy_logs.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  username|device_id|date|consumption_kwh
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  john_doe|1|2024-11-01|0.5
  john_doe|2|2024-11-01|2.3
  john_doe|1|2024-11-02|0.6
  jane_smith|3|2024-11-01|0.2
  ```

### 6. Activity Logs
- **File Name**: `activity_logs.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  username|timestamp|device_id|action|details
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  john_doe|2024-11-01 07:00:00|1|Power On|Automation triggered: Morning Lights
  john_doe|2024-11-01 08:30:00|2|Settings Changed|Temperature set to 72
  jane_smith|2024-11-01 09:15:00|3|Power On|Manual control
  ```

All files will be saved in the `data` directory to ensure organization and easy access. The format uses a pipe (`|`) delimiter for better readability and parsing. Different types of data will be isolated to ensure efficient data management and retrieval.


=== design_spec.md ===
# SmartHomeManager Web Application Design Specification

## Overview
This Flask web application manages smart home devices, their activity and energy logs, and automation rules, providing a comprehensive UI for user navigation.

---

## Data Files
- `users.txt`
  - Format: username|email
  - Example: jane_smith|jane@example.com
- `devices.txt`
  - Format: username|device_id|device_name|device_type|room|brand|model|status|power|brightness|temperature|mode|schedule_time
  - Example: john_doe|1|Thermostat|Thermostat|Bedroom|Nest|Learning|Online|on||72|Auto|22:00
- `rooms.txt`
  - Format: username|room_id|room_name
  - Example: john_doe|2|Bedroom
- `energy_logs.txt`
  - Format: username|date|device_id|consumption_kwh
  - Example: john_doe|2024-11-01|1|0.5
- `activity_logs.txt`
  - Format: username|timestamp|device_id|action|details
  - Example: john_doe|2024-11-01 08:30:00|2|Changed|Temperature
- `automation_rules.txt`
  - Format: username|rule_id|rule_name|trigger_type|trigger_value|action_device_id|action_type|action_value|enabled
  - Example: john_doe|1|Morning Lights|Time|07:00|3|Turn On|Light|true

---

## Flask Routes and Pages

### 1. Dashboard Page
- URL: `/dashboard`
- Methods: GET
- Description: Displays summary of user's devices and quick navigation buttons.
- Data shown:
  - User's devices list (from devices.txt)
  - Navigation buttons to Devices List, Energy Summary, Activity Logs, Automation Rules
- Template: `dashboard.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Dashboard - SmartHomeManager</h1>`
  - Navigation Buttons:
    - Devices List (`id="device-list-button"`)
    - Energy Summary (`id="energy-button"`)
    - Activity Logs (`id="activity-button"`)
    - Automation Rules (`id="automation-button"`)

### 2. Devices List Page
- URL: `/devices`
- Methods: GET
- Description: Displays a table listing all user's devices with device details.
- Data shown:
  - Devices data from `devices.txt`
- Template: `device_list_page.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Devices List</h1>`
  - Table (`id="device-table"`) with columns:
    - Device ID, Name, Type, Room, Brand, Model, Status, Power, Brightness, Temperature, Mode, Schedule Time
  - Buttons:
    - Add Device (`id="add-device-button"`) navigates to Add Device page
    - Back to Dashboard (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)

### 3. Device Details Page
- URL: `/device/<int:device_id>`
- Methods: GET
- Description: Details and control page for a specific device.
- Data shown:
  - Device info from `devices.txt`
  - Current status and controls (e.g., On/Off toggle, Brightness slider)
- Template: `device_detail.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Device Details - {{ device_name }}</h1>`
  - Device data fields displayed
  - Control Buttons:
    - Power Toggle (`id="control-device-button-{{ device_id }}"`)
    - Save Settings (`id="save-settings-button"`)
    - Back to Devices List (`id="back-to-devices"`)

### 4. Energy Summary Page
- URL: `/energy-summary`
- Methods: GET
- Description: Displays energy consumption per device and overall.
- Data shown:
  - Energy consumption from `energy_logs.txt`
- Template: `energy_summary.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Energy Summary</h1>`
  - Table (`id="energy-table"`): Username, Date, Device ID, Consumption (kWh)
  - Back Button (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)

### 5. Activity Logs Page
- URL: `/activity-logs`
- Methods: GET
- Description: Shows device activity logs.
- Data shown:
  - Activity logs from `activity_logs.txt`
- Template: `activity_logs.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Activity Logs</h1>`
  - Table (`id="activity-table"`): Username, Timestamp, Device ID, Action, Details
  - Date filter Input (`id="date-filter"`)
  - Apply Filter Button (`id="apply-filter-button"`)
  - Back Button (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)

### 6. Automation Rules Page
- URL: `/automation-rules`
- Methods: GET, POST
- Description: Manage automation rules.
- Data shown/edited:
  - Rules from `automation_rules.txt`
- Template: `automation_rules.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Automation Rules</h1>`
  - Table (`id="rules-table"`): Rule ID, Name, Trigger Type, Trigger Value, Action Device ID, Action Type, Action Value, Enabled
  - Add Rule Button (`id="add-rule-button"`)
  - Save Settings Button (`id="save-settings-button"`)
  - Back Button (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)

### 7. Add Device Page
- URL: `/add-device`
- Methods: GET, POST
- Description: Form to add new device.
- Template: `add_device_page.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Add New Device</h1>`
  - Input fields correspond to device data fields
  - Submit Button (`id="submit-device-button"`)
  - Back Button (`id="back-to-devices"`)

---

## Templates Plan

All templates rendered with variables passed from Flask. Use `url_for()` for linking.

1. `dashboard.html` - Summary view with navigation buttons.
2. `device_list_page.html` - Devices table and navigation.
3. `device_detail.html` - Detailed device info and control buttons.
4. `energy_summary.html` - Energy consumption report table.
5. `activity_logs.html` - Activity logs with filter inputs.
6. `automation_rules.html` - Rules list and controls.
7. `add_device_page.html` - Form for adding devices.

---

## Flask Route and Function Names

- `dashboard()` -> `/dashboard`
- `device_list()` -> `/devices`
- `device_detail(device_id: int)` -> `/device/<int:device_id>`
- `energy_summary()` -> `/energy-summary`
- `activity_logs()` -> `/activity-logs`
- `automation_rules()` -> `/automation-rules`
- `add_device()` -> `/add-device`

---

## UI Element IDs Summary

- Buttons:
  - `device-list-button`
  - `energy-button`
  - `activity-button`
  - `automation-button`
  - `add-device-button`
  - `back-to-dashboard`
  - `back-to-devices`
  - `save-settings-button`
  - `submit-device-button`
  - `apply-filter-button`
  - `add-rule-button`
  - `control-device-button-{device_id}` (dynamic)
- Tables:
  - `device-table`
  - `energy-table`
  - `activity-table`
  - `rules-table`
- Inputs:
  - `date-filter`

---

This design specification meets the requirements for a Flask web application, providing clear route definitions, templates, UI elements with consistent IDs, and aligns with provided data file formats and content.

  [user_task_description] # Requirements Document for 'SmartHomeManager' Web Application  ## 1. Objective Develop a comprehensive web application named 'SmartHomeManager' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to manage smart home devices, control them remotely, set automation rules, and monitor energy consumption. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.  ## 2. Language The required development language for the 'SmartHomeManager' application is Python.  ## 3. Page Design  The 'SmartHomeManager' web application will consist of the following seven pages:  ### 1. Dashboard Page - **Page Title**: Smart Home Dashboard - **Overview**: The main hub displaying overview of all devices, quick controls, and navigation to all functionality. - **Elements**:   - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.   - **ID: device-summary** - Type: Div - Summary showing total devices, active devices, and offline devices count.   - **ID: device-list-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to device list page.   - **ID: add-device-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to add device page.   - **ID: automation-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to automation rules page.   - **ID: energy-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to energy report page.   - **ID: activity-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to activity logs page.   - **ID: room-list** - Type: Div - List of all rooms with device counts, displayed as a dashboard section.  ### 2. Device List Page - **Page Title**: My Devices - **Overview**: A page displaying all registered smart devices with their status and quick controls. - **Elements**:   - **ID: device-list-page** - Type: Div - Container for the device list page.   - **ID: device-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying all devices with name, type, room, status, and actions.   - **ID: control-device-button-{device_id}** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to device control page (each row has this button).   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 3. Add Device Page - **Page Title**: Add New Device - **Overview**: A page for users to register a new smart device. - **Elements**:   - **ID: add-device-page** - Type: Div - Container for the add device page.   - **ID: device-name** - Type: Input - Field to input device name.   - **ID: device-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select device type (Light, Thermostat, Camera, Lock, Sensor, Appliance).   - **ID: device-room** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select room (Living Room, Bedroom, Kitchen, Bathroom, Garage).   - **ID: submit-device-button** - Type: Button - Button to submit the new device.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 4. Device Control Page - **Page Title**: Device Control - **Overview**: A page for controlling a specific device with detailed settings. - **Elements**:   - **ID: device-control-page** - Type: Div - Container for the device control page.   - **ID: device-name-display** - Type: H2 - Display device name.   - **ID: device-status-display** - Type: Div - Display current device status (Online/Offline).   - **ID: power-toggle** - Type: Button - Button to toggle device power on/off.   - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save device settings.   - **ID: back-to-devices** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to device list.  ### 5. Automation Rules Page - **Page Title**: Automation Rules - **Overview**: A page for creating and managing automation rules for devices. - **Elements**:   - **ID: automation-page** - Type: Div - Container for the automation rules page.   - **ID: rules-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying all automation rules with name, trigger, action, and status.   - **ID: rule-name** - Type: Input - Field to input rule name.   - **ID: trigger-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select trigger type (Time, Motion, Temperature).   - **ID: trigger-value** - Type: Input - Field to input trigger value (e.g., time or threshold).   - **ID: action-device** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select target device.   - **ID: action-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select action type (Turn On, Turn Off, Set Brightness, Set Temperature).   - **ID: add-rule-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new automation rule.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 6. Energy Report Page - **Page Title**: Energy Report - **Overview**: A page displaying energy consumption data and statistics for all devices. - **Elements**:   - **ID: energy-page** - Type: Div - Container for the energy report page.   - **ID: energy-summary** - Type: Div - Summary showing total energy consumption and cost estimate.   - **ID: energy-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying energy consumption per device with date and kWh.   - **ID: date-filter** - Type: Input (date) - Field to filter energy data by date.   - **ID: apply-filter-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply date filter.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ### 7. Activity Logs Page - **Page Title**: Activity Logs - **Overview**: A page displaying all device activity logs and system events. - **Elements**:   - **ID: activity-page** - Type: Div - Container for the activity logs page.   - **ID: activity-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying activity logs with timestamp, device, action, and details.   - **ID: search-activity** - Type: Input - Field to search activity logs.   - **ID: apply-search-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply search filter.   - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.  ## 4. Data Storage  The 'SmartHomeManager' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:  ### 1. User Data - **File Name**: `users.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|email   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|john@example.com   jane_smith|jane@example.com   ```  ### 2. Device Data - **File Name**: `devices.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|device_id|device_name|device_type|room|brand|model|status|power|brightness|temperature|mode|schedule_time   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|1|Living Room Light|Light|Living Room|Philips|Hue|Online|on|75|||Auto|   john_doe|2|Bedroom Thermostat|Thermostat|Bedroom|Nest|Learning|Online|on||72|Auto|22:00   jane_smith|3|Kitchen Camera|Camera|Kitchen|Ring|Indoor|Online|on||||Manual|   ```  ### 3. Room Data - **File Name**: `rooms.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|room_id|room_name   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|1|Living Room   john_doe|2|Bedroom   john_doe|3|Kitchen   jane_smith|1|Living Room   ```  ### 4. Automation Rules Data - **File Name**: `automation_rules.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|rule_id|rule_name|trigger_type|trigger_value|action_device_id|action_type|action_value|enabled   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|1|Morning Lights|Time|07:00|1|Turn On||true   john_doe|2|Night Mode|Time|22:00|1|Set Brightness|20|true   jane_smith|1|Motion Alert|Motion|detected|3|Turn On||true   ```  ### 5. Energy Consumption Logs - **File Name**: `energy_logs.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|device_id|date|consumption_kwh   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|1|2024-11-01|0.5   john_doe|2|2024-11-01|2.3   john_doe|1|2024-11-02|0.6   jane_smith|3|2024-11-01|0.2   ```  ### 6. Activity Logs - **File Name**: `activity_logs.txt` - **Data Format**:   ```   username|timestamp|device_id|action|details   ``` - **Example Data**:   ```   john_doe|2024-11-01 07:00:00|1|Power On|Automation triggered: Morning Lights   john_doe|2024-11-01 08:30:00|2|Settings Changed|Temperature set to 72   jane_smith|2024-11-01 09:15:00|3|Power On|Manual control   ```  All files will be saved in the `data` directory to ensure organization and easy access. The format uses a pipe (`|`) delimiter for better readability and parsing. Different types of data will be isolated to ensure efficient data management and retrieval.

Energy Summary Page - URL: `/energy-summary` - Methods: GET - Description: Displays energy consumption per device and overall. - Data shown:   - Energy consumption from `energy_logs.txt` - Template: `energy_summary.html` - Elements:   - Header `<h1>Energy Summary</h1>`   - Table (`id="energy-table"`): Username, Date, Device ID, Consumption (kWh)   - Back Button (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)  ### 5. Activity Logs Page - URL: `/activity-logs` - Methods: GET - Description: Shows device activity logs. - Data shown:   - Activity logs from `activity_logs.txt` - Template: `activity_logs.html` - Elements:   - Header `<h1>Activity Logs</h1>`   - Table (`id="activity-table"`): Username, Timestamp, Device ID, Action, Details   - Date filter Input (`id="date-filter"`)   - Apply Filter Button (`id="apply-filter-button"`)   - Back Button (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)  ### 6. Automation Rules Page - URL: `/automation-rules` - Methods: GET, POST - Description: Manage automation rules. - Data shown/edited:   - Rules from `automation_rules.txt` - Template: `automation_rules.html` - Elements:   - Header `<h1>Automation Rules</h1>`   - Table (`id="rules-table"`): Rule ID, Name, Trigger Type, Trigger Value, Action Device ID, Action Type, Action Value, Enabled   - Add Rule Button (`id="add-rule-button"`)   - Save Settings Button (`id="save-settings-button"`)   - Back Button (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)  ### 7. Add Device Page - URL: `/add-device` - Methods: GET, POST - Description: Form to add new device. - Template: `add_device_page.html` - Elements:   - Header `<h1>Add New Device</h1>`   - Input fields correspond to device data fields   - Submit Button (`id="submit-device-button"`)   - Back Button (`id="back-to-devices"`)  ---  ## Templates Plan  All templates rendered with variables passed from Flask. Use `url_for()` for linking.  1. `dashboard.html` - Summary view with navigation buttons. 2. `device_list_page.html` - Devices table and navigation. 3. `device_detail.html` - Detailed device info and control buttons. 4. `energy_summary.html` - Energy consumption report table. 5. `activity_logs.html` - Activity logs with filter inputs. 6. `automation_rules.html` - Rules list and controls. 7. `add_device_page.html` - Form for adding devices.  ---  ## Flask Route and Function Names  - `dashboard()` -> `/dashboard` - `device_list()` -> `/devices` - `device_detail(device_id: int)` -> `/device/<int:device_id>` - `energy_summary()` -> `/energy-summary` - `activity_logs()` -> `/activity-logs` - `automation_rules()` -> `/automation-rules` - `add_device()` -> `/add-device`  ---  ## UI Element IDs Summary  - Buttons:   - `device-list-button`   - `energy-button`   - `activity-button`   - `automation-button`   - `add-device-button`   - `back-to-dashboard`   - `back-to-devices`   - `save-settings-button`   - `submit-device-button`   - `apply-filter-button`   - `add-rule-button`   - `control-device-button-{device_id}` (dynamic) - Tables:   - `device-table`   - `energy-table`   - `activity-table`   - `rules-table` - Inputs:   - `date-filter`  ---  This design specification meets the requirements for a Flask web application, providing clear route definitions, templates, UI elements with consistent IDs, and aligns with provided data file formats and content.

=== LLM_call: FrontendDeveloper ===
INPUT: === user_task_description ===

# Requirements Document for 'SmartHomeManager' Web Application

## 1. Objective
Develop a comprehensive web application named 'SmartHomeManager' using Python, with data managed through local text files. The application enables users to manage smart home devices, control them remotely, set automation rules, and monitor energy consumption. Note that the website should start from the Dashboard page.

## 2. Language
The required development language for the 'SmartHomeManager' application is Python.

## 3. Page Design

The 'SmartHomeManager' web application will consist of the following seven pages:

### 1. Dashboard Page
- **Page Title**: Smart Home Dashboard
- **Overview**: The main hub displaying overview of all devices, quick controls, and navigation to all functionality.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: dashboard-page** - Type: Div - Container for the dashboard page.
  - **ID: device-summary** - Type: Div - Summary showing total devices, active devices, and offline devices count.
  - **ID: device-list-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to device list page.
  - **ID: add-device-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to add device page.
  - **ID: automation-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to automation rules page.
  - **ID: energy-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to energy report page.
  - **ID: activity-button** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to activity logs page.
  - **ID: room-list** - Type: Div - List of all rooms with device counts, displayed as a dashboard section.

### 2. Device List Page
- **Page Title**: My Devices
- **Overview**: A page displaying all registered smart devices with their status and quick controls.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: device-list-page** - Type: Div - Container for the device list page.
  - **ID: device-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying all devices with name, type, room, status, and actions.
  - **ID: control-device-button-{device_id}** - Type: Button - Button to navigate to device control page (each row has this button).
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 3. Add Device Page
- **Page Title**: Add New Device
- **Overview**: A page for users to register a new smart device.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: add-device-page** - Type: Div - Container for the add device page.
  - **ID: device-name** - Type: Input - Field to input device name.
  - **ID: device-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select device type (Light, Thermostat, Camera, Lock, Sensor, Appliance).
  - **ID: device-room** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select room (Living Room, Bedroom, Kitchen, Bathroom, Garage).
  - **ID: submit-device-button** - Type: Button - Button to submit the new device.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 4. Device Control Page
- **Page Title**: Device Control
- **Overview**: A page for controlling a specific device with detailed settings.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: device-control-page** - Type: Div - Container for the device control page.
  - **ID: device-name-display** - Type: H2 - Display device name.
  - **ID: device-status-display** - Type: Div - Display current device status (Online/Offline).
  - **ID: power-toggle** - Type: Button - Button to toggle device power on/off.
  - **ID: save-settings-button** - Type: Button - Button to save device settings.
  - **ID: back-to-devices** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to device list.

### 5. Automation Rules Page
- **Page Title**: Automation Rules
- **Overview**: A page for creating and managing automation rules for devices.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: automation-page** - Type: Div - Container for the automation rules page.
  - **ID: rules-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying all automation rules with name, trigger, action, and status.
  - **ID: rule-name** - Type: Input - Field to input rule name.
  - **ID: trigger-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select trigger type (Time, Motion, Temperature).
  - **ID: trigger-value** - Type: Input - Field to input trigger value (e.g., time or threshold).
  - **ID: action-device** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select target device.
  - **ID: action-type** - Type: Dropdown - Dropdown to select action type (Turn On, Turn Off, Set Brightness, Set Temperature).
  - **ID: add-rule-button** - Type: Button - Button to add new automation rule.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 6. Energy Report Page
- **Page Title**: Energy Report
- **Overview**: A page displaying energy consumption data and statistics for all devices.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: energy-page** - Type: Div - Container for the energy report page.
  - **ID: energy-summary** - Type: Div - Summary showing total energy consumption and cost estimate.
  - **ID: energy-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying energy consumption per device with date and kWh.
  - **ID: date-filter** - Type: Input (date) - Field to filter energy data by date.
  - **ID: apply-filter-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply date filter.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

### 7. Activity Logs Page
- **Page Title**: Activity Logs
- **Overview**: A page displaying all device activity logs and system events.
- **Elements**:
  - **ID: activity-page** - Type: Div - Container for the activity logs page.
  - **ID: activity-table** - Type: Table - Table displaying activity logs with timestamp, device, action, and details.
  - **ID: search-activity** - Type: Input - Field to search activity logs.
  - **ID: apply-search-button** - Type: Button - Button to apply search filter.
  - **ID: back-to-dashboard** - Type: Button - Button to navigate back to dashboard.

## 4. Data Storage

The 'SmartHomeManager' application will store data locally in text files organized in the directory 'data'. The following data formats and examples are defined:

### 1. User Data
- **File Name**: `users.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  username|email
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  john_doe|john@example.com
  jane_smith|jane@example.com
  ```

### 2. Device Data
- **File Name**: `devices.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  username|device_id|device_name|device_type|room|brand|model|status|power|brightness|temperature|mode|schedule_time
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  john_doe|1|Living Room Light|Light|Living Room|Philips|Hue|Online|on|75|||Auto|
  john_doe|2|Bedroom Thermostat|Thermostat|Bedroom|Nest|Learning|Online|on||72|Auto|22:00
  jane_smith|3|Kitchen Camera|Camera|Kitchen|Ring|Indoor|Online|on||||Manual|
  ```

### 3. Room Data
- **File Name**: `rooms.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  username|room_id|room_name
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  john_doe|1|Living Room
  john_doe|2|Bedroom
  john_doe|3|Kitchen
  jane_smith|1|Living Room
  ```

### 4. Automation Rules Data
- **File Name**: `automation_rules.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  username|rule_id|rule_name|trigger_type|trigger_value|action_device_id|action_type|action_value|enabled
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  john_doe|1|Morning Lights|Time|07:00|1|Turn On||true
  john_doe|2|Night Mode|Time|22:00|1|Set Brightness|20|true
  jane_smith|1|Motion Alert|Motion|detected|3|Turn On||true
  ```

### 5. Energy Consumption Logs
- **File Name**: `energy_logs.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  username|device_id|date|consumption_kwh
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  john_doe|1|2024-11-01|0.5
  john_doe|2|2024-11-01|2.3
  john_doe|1|2024-11-02|0.6
  jane_smith|3|2024-11-01|0.2
  ```

### 6. Activity Logs
- **File Name**: `activity_logs.txt`
- **Data Format**:
  ```
  username|timestamp|device_id|action|details
  ```
- **Example Data**:
  ```
  john_doe|2024-11-01 07:00:00|1|Power On|Automation triggered: Morning Lights
  john_doe|2024-11-01 08:30:00|2|Settings Changed|Temperature set to 72
  jane_smith|2024-11-01 09:15:00|3|Power On|Manual control
  ```

All files will be saved in the `data` directory to ensure organization and easy access. The format uses a pipe (`|`) delimiter for better readability and parsing. Different types of data will be isolated to ensure efficient data management and retrieval.


=== design_spec.md ===
# SmartHomeManager Web Application Design Specification

## Overview
This Flask web application manages smart home devices, their activity and energy logs, and automation rules, providing a comprehensive UI for user navigation.

---

## Data Files
- `users.txt`
  - Format: username|email
  - Example: jane_smith|jane@example.com
- `devices.txt`
  - Format: username|device_id|device_name|device_type|room|brand|model|status|power|brightness|temperature|mode|schedule_time
  - Example: john_doe|1|Thermostat|Thermostat|Bedroom|Nest|Learning|Online|on||72|Auto|22:00
- `rooms.txt`
  - Format: username|room_id|room_name
  - Example: john_doe|2|Bedroom
- `energy_logs.txt`
  - Format: username|date|device_id|consumption_kwh
  - Example: john_doe|2024-11-01|1|0.5
- `activity_logs.txt`
  - Format: username|timestamp|device_id|action|details
  - Example: john_doe|2024-11-01 08:30:00|2|Changed|Temperature
- `automation_rules.txt`
  - Format: username|rule_id|rule_name|trigger_type|trigger_value|action_device_id|action_type|action_value|enabled
  - Example: john_doe|1|Morning Lights|Time|07:00|3|Turn On|Light|true

---

## Flask Routes and Pages

### 1. Dashboard Page
- URL: `/dashboard`
- Methods: GET
- Description: Displays summary of user's devices and quick navigation buttons.
- Data shown:
  - User's devices list (from devices.txt)
  - Navigation buttons to Devices List, Energy Summary, Activity Logs, Automation Rules
- Template: `dashboard.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Dashboard - SmartHomeManager</h1>`
  - Navigation Buttons:
    - Devices List (`id="device-list-button"`)
    - Energy Summary (`id="energy-button"`)
    - Activity Logs (`id="activity-button"`)
    - Automation Rules (`id="automation-button"`)

### 2. Devices List Page
- URL: `/devices`
- Methods: GET
- Description: Displays a table listing all user's devices with device details.
- Data shown:
  - Devices data from `devices.txt`
- Template: `device_list_page.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Devices List</h1>`
  - Table (`id="device-table"`) with columns:
    - Device ID, Name, Type, Room, Brand, Model, Status, Power, Brightness, Temperature, Mode, Schedule Time
  - Buttons:
    - Add Device (`id="add-device-button"`) navigates to Add Device page
    - Back to Dashboard (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)

### 3. Device Details Page
- URL: `/device/<int:device_id>`
- Methods: GET
- Description: Details and control page for a specific device.
- Data shown:
  - Device info from `devices.txt`
  - Current status and controls (e.g., On/Off toggle, Brightness slider)
- Template: `device_detail.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Device Details - {{ device_name }}</h1>`
  - Device data fields displayed
  - Control Buttons:
    - Power Toggle (`id="control-device-button-{{ device_id }}"`)
    - Save Settings (`id="save-settings-button"`)
    - Back to Devices List (`id="back-to-devices"`)

### 4. Energy Summary Page
- URL: `/energy-summary`
- Methods: GET
- Description: Displays energy consumption per device and overall.
- Data shown:
  - Energy consumption from `energy_logs.txt`
- Template: `energy_summary.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Energy Summary</h1>`
  - Table (`id="energy-table"`): Username, Date, Device ID, Consumption (kWh)
  - Back Button (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)

### 5. Activity Logs Page
- URL: `/activity-logs`
- Methods: GET
- Description: Shows device activity logs.
- Data shown:
  - Activity logs from `activity_logs.txt`
- Template: `activity_logs.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Activity Logs</h1>`
  - Table (`id="activity-table"`): Username, Timestamp, Device ID, Action, Details
  - Date filter Input (`id="date-filter"`)
  - Apply Filter Button (`id="apply-filter-button"`)
  - Back Button (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)

### 6. Automation Rules Page
- URL: `/automation-rules`
- Methods: GET, POST
- Description: Manage automation rules.
- Data shown/edited:
  - Rules from `automation_rules.txt`
- Template: `automation_rules.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Automation Rules</h1>`
  - Table (`id="rules-table"`): Rule ID, Name, Trigger Type, Trigger Value, Action Device ID, Action Type, Action Value, Enabled
  - Add Rule Button (`id="add-rule-button"`)
  - Save Settings Button (`id="save-settings-button"`)
  - Back Button (`id="back-to-dashboard"`)

### 7. Add Device Page
- URL: `/add-device`
- Methods: GET, POST
- Description: Form to add new device.
- Template: `add_device_page.html`
- Elements:
  - Header `<h1>Add New Device</h1>`
  - Input fields correspond to device data fields
  - Submit Button (`id="submit-device-button"`)
  - Back Button (`id="back-to-devices"`)

---

## Templates Plan

All templates rendered with variables passed from Flask. Use `url_for()` for linking.

1. `dashboard.html` - Summary view with navigation buttons.
2. `device_list_page.html` - Devices table and navigation.
3. `device_detail.html` - Detailed device info and control buttons.
4. `energy_summary.html` - Energy consumption report table.
5. `activity_logs.html` - Activity logs with filter inputs.
6. `automation_rules.html` - Rules list and controls.
7. `add_device_page.html` - Form for adding devices.

---

## Flask Route and Function Names

- `dashboard()` -> `/dashboard`
- `device_list()` -> `/devices`
- `device_detail(device_id: int)` -> `/device/<int:device_id>`
- `energy_summary()` -> `/energy-summary`
- `activity_logs()` -> `/activity-logs`
- `automation_rules()` -> `/automation-rules`
- `add_device()` -> `/add-device`

---

## UI Element IDs Summary

- Buttons:
  - `device-list-button`
  - `energy-button`
  - `activity-button`
  - `automation-button`
  - `add-device-button`
  - `back-to-dashboard`
  - `back-to-devices`
  - `save-settings-button`
  - `submit-device-button`
  - `apply-filter-button`
  - `add-rule-button`
  - `control-device-button-{device_id}` (dynamic)
- Tables:
  - `device-table`
  - `energy-table`
  - `activity-table`
  - `rules-table`
- Inputs:
  - `date-filter`

---

This design specification meets the requirements for a Flask web application, providing clear route definitions, templates, UI elements with consistent IDs, and aligns with provided data file formats and content.
